text: The surgeon identifies a four-centimeter hilar tumor with close vascular contact involving two arteries, two veins, and the urinary tract. The incision is made through the Gerota's fascia, following the psoas muscle. The procedure is performed using the DaVinci XI robotic system with four arms.
The surgeon uses a vessel loop to control the posterior branch of the vein and two arteries, while managing surrounding fat to improve visibility in the operating field.

The surgeon frees the tumor from the pelvic region, continuing the posterior dissection until the anterior branch of the vein is visible. A small artery supplying the tumor is clipped, and the dissection proceeds to separate the tumor from the vein.

The surgeon performs a nuclear resection of the tumor without clamping, placing the excised tumor into a specimen bag. Hemostasis is achieved using a 3-0 monofilament running suture to control bleeding.
